title: What happens with adams soul during third impact
display_title: What happens with adams soul during third impact
wikitext:
[]: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by orcot » Fri Aug 07, 2020 8:32 am Like the question states, What do you believe happenend with adam's soul during the third impact. I assume with the kaworu dummy plug being a thing theirs probably a vat somewhere with naked young boys. When Shinji popped kaworu, his soul probably was placed in one of these clone bodies. So what happenend with him can Giant Naked Rei claim adam's soul she already had a part of his flesh trough Gendo at this time. I feel that she somehow did but don't feel that she was suppose to be able to.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Electreel » Sat Aug 08, 2020 4:44 pm orcot wrote: When Shinji popped kaworu, his soul probably was placed in one of these clone bodies.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by orcot » Sun Aug 09, 2020 2:48 pm Electreel wrote: That's assuming SEELE would have cared to re-salvage Adam's soul into a new Kaworu body, which would have essentially reversed the killing of the 17th Angel and set things back to square one.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by AWinters » Fri Aug 14, 2020 8:13 pm I'm more confused about what happened to his body. He's an embryo, yet they've managed to make Evas out of him.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Melkor » Fri Aug 14, 2020 9:06 pm AWinters wrote: I'm more confused about what happened to his body. He's an embryo, yet they've managed to make Evas out of him.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 15, 2020 6:21 am Those who left the base before the experiment took tissue samples, I would assume.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Melkor » Sun Aug 16, 2020 12:30 am I guess that makes sense. In the Rebuilds there's multiple places they could have gotten the Adam DNA from to make the Evas, like from the Third Angel that was in captivity or from any of the four Adam's bodies they discovered, while in the original series the only window of time in which they could have obtained it would have been during the expedition.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Aug 16, 2020 5:39 pm Melkor wrote: That's a good question. Come to think of it, where did Nerv even get the Adam DNA to make EVAs like Unit-00 and Unit-02 that are clones of Adam if Adam was completely destroyed during Second Impact and the Adam embryo wasn't discovered and brought to them by Kaji until recently?

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 16, 2020 6:06 pm Lavinius wrote: IIRC it's not exactly clear in 08 that Kaji has stolen the embryo at all

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Aug 16, 2020 6:59 pm Reichu wrote: When Kaji talks on the phone with Gendo around when Gaghiel attacks, you can see a suitcase in the foreground. This same suitcase is opened at the end of the episode and it has the embryo inside.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 16, 2020 7:41 pm Lavinius wrote: Clearly. I mean that you could think Kaji was just acting as a covert delivery boy- secret from the crew of the ships, but willingly sent by Kiel to Gendou. My point here, in case it wasn't clear, is that from the Doylean perspective, it doesn't seem that Kaji was thought of as having stolen the embryo when episode 08 was written.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Aug 16, 2020 8:52 pm Reichu wrote: An official transfer doesn't make sense of the secrecy that Kaji is operating under. Kaji is introduced in episode 7 (voice only) as an illicit operator doing dirty work for Gendo; and he continues to do sketchy things all the way until his death. That's just what Kaji does. Had Kiel wanted to send the embryo to Gendo, it could have been accomplished through official, and FAR more secure, channels. No need for all that needless risk. Seele didn't care enough about Eva-02's transfer to make sure it had so much as a power outlet at sea -- that was entirely Misato's doing!

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 17, 2020 3:09 am On the plus side, this feeds nicely into my head canon conspiracy that Kiel has a personal beef against Eva-02 and would have probably been pleased to see her get eaten by Gaghiel. In fact, WHAT IF he made his Eva Series a bunch of similarly eyeless things with big mouths specifically so that he could get a chance to see that fantasy at last play out? Yes... yes, this is good....

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Melkor » Mon Aug 17, 2020 3:53 am Reichu wrote: On the plus side, this feeds nicely into my head canon conspiracy that Kiel has a personal beef against Eva-02 and would have probably been pleased to see her get eaten by Gaghiel. In fact, WHAT IF he made his Eva Series a bunch of similarly eyeless things with big mouths specifically so that he could get a chance to see that fantasy at last play out? Yes... yes, this is good....

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 17, 2020 4:11 am Oh, it's basically a bunch of completely substanceless silliness I came up with years ago for fanfic purposes. His comment about Eva-02 being "loathsome" (or maybe he's just talking about Evas in general, but it's more interesting if he's talking about Eva-02 specifically), and the brutal indignities he allowed his servant MP Evas to bring upon her, stirred the imagination quite a bit. It made me wonder if Eva-02 could possibly have ever done anything to make Kiel hate her personally. One completely unexplained aspect of Kiel's character is the fact that most of his body is replaced with cybernetics. Kiel looked pretty normal in 2001, and was moving out and about in public, which made me think that some kind of traumatic incident must have happened between then and 2015. What's good for producing a traumatic incident? A crazed Evangelion. In the present day, Eva-02 is rather well-behaved, but that doesn't mean this was always the case. So I came up with the idea of Eva-02 going berserk immediately after the procedure for suturing Kyoko's soul back together. Have Kiel visiting Gehirn on that unfortunate day, bam, he gets nearly crushed to death and his life can only be saved through the extensive use of prosthetic organs. He decides to let that Zeppelin bitch stay in the Eva so she can be subjected to brutal advances in restraint tech. And if anything bad were ever to happen to Eva-02 later on, he would be the last person to complain. So yeah, just something I use to entertain myself. Not meant to have any kind of deeper validity, though if other people want to write Kiel as being reasonably bitter that Asuka's crazy mom fucked his shit up, I won't complain or anything.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Melkor » Mon Aug 17, 2020 3:24 pm I think there could be something to that theory. Seele seems to have a lot of connections to Germany. Seele, Gehirn, and Nerv are all German words. There are two Nerv bases in Germany, which is a lot for a country that small, which hints at it's importance. Kiel is a German name, and I'm willing to bet that a lot of Seele's members are probably German too, and that Seele might have even been founded in Germany. Germany has a history with secret societies. The Bavarian Illuminati was founded in Germany in the 1700's, and Seele seems like an organization in the same vein as the Illuminati. With Seele having such heavy influence in Germany, is it possible that Kyoko might have even been a member, or connected to one of its members like Yui was, and betrayed them? Maybe Kyoko and Kiel had a thing going on at some point, like Gendo and Naoko did.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Aug 17, 2020 7:27 pm I'm not sure if the amount of German names in the show carries as many implications as you make it out to be. Scifi anime in general, and mecha in particular absolutely LOVES its gratuitious German for names; there is a metric fuckton of garbage named after more or less random German words, ranging back way before Eva. Sure, there are definitely some observations to be made, but not everything loops back to Germany.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 18, 2020 3:10 am Yeah, honestly, the reasoning for my head canon basically goes no further than: 1) If you squint a bit, it kind of looks like Kiel uniquely despises Eva-02. 2) Kiel needs something to handily explain why he's so extensively cyberneticized despite being apparently fine in 2001. 3) Kyoko is crazy, but she's strangely well-behaved as an Eva. Maybe that wasn't always the case. Maybe something bad happened before they realized they needed to restrain the shit out of her. 4) Have Kiel visit one of the German Gehirn installations while Kyoko is flipping out; make sure he gets thoroughly crushed; have him vow to make Dr. Zeppelin suffer extensively. Fanfiction logic, really. Though with how much utterly sadistic abuse he has the MPEs subject Eva-02 to despite at least pretending (badly) to be a gentleman at moments like Ritsuko's, er, interrogation ... I dunno, man. He might as well have a raging hate-boner for Asuka's mom. Then again, he sees fit to let his own shitty Evas get thoroughly mangled and abused, so maybe he just has a hate-boner for Adam and her descendants in general, and was thoroughly disappointed that Kaworu's death was a mere insta-crushing as opposed to him being slowly ripped apart or something. There, Kaworu. Back on topic, I guess. More on topic: what happens with Adam's soul during Third Impact? Well, see, she borrows Rei's body for a little bit, pulls some Angel souls out of her pockets, and figuratively straps on the Spear of Longinus so she can fuck those Angel souls right into Shinji's mom. Yui likes it, too. Then she uses the Kaworu avatar to say some vague nonsense, and hitches a ride on the Yui boat along with all the other cool people. Once they reach the promised land, both Yui and Adam will get to achieve their outstanding dreams of being house planetwives together.

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Analyzor » Wed Aug 19, 2020 11:31 am Melkor wrote: That's a good question. Come to think of it, where did Nerv even get the Adam DNA to make EVAs like Unit-00 and Unit-02 that are clones of Adam if Adam was completely destroyed during Second Impact and the Adam embryo wasn't discovered and brought to them by Kaji until recently?

[]: Re: What happens with adam's soul during third impact             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 19, 2020 12:05 pm A team left Antarctica "with the research" right before Second Impact. Them taking tissue samples directly from the gigantic humanoid being that is right there is much, much simpler than them scraping bits of billions-years-old injuries off the shaft of a spear. They could have collected tissue at leisure, of the best quality, from all around Adam's body. It would make no sense not to do it. The spear being sent to the Dead Sea and back is an assumption. All we know is that *A* spear is sent to Antarctica from the Dead Sea. The implied significance of the Dead Sea is the Dead Sea Scrolls -- a connection is being made between the two. Since the spears possess the ability to make decisions in contexts pertaining to Seeds of Life and Impacts and such, that means they contain information. They can also respond to the will of the one who wields them, meaning they can receive information from the outside, making it also possible they can send information the opposite way. In other words, there was a spear -- not the one Adam was impaled on, but a different one -- that was located in the Dead Sea area that inspired the mystics who wrote the scrolls. Consider that if this "spear sent from the Dead Sea" bit of dialogue is not treated as a hint regarding the scrolls, there is absolutely NOTHING in the show at all that even attempts to address the why and how of their existence. So this is actually pretty important. This clue can be put to incredible use to illuminate a mystery that otherwise receives no illumination at all, or it can be wasted on a series of actions that make no sense. Getting in and out of Antarctica is not easy. The White Moon already has an incredibly high-tech research facility right inside it. Seele have complete control over this facility; the leader of Seele, Kiel Lorenz himself, is hanging out there. Anything that Seele needs to accomplish before Second Impact can be accomplished right on the premises. What possible reason could Seele have to send a gigantic, highly classified objects through treacherous polar conditions, all the way to a political garbage fire like the West Bank (I'm sure lots of people there would be real happy about Seele flaunting their discovery of something named after a sacred Biblical object), and then BACK AGAIN? If for some reason they HAD to send the Spear away, the West Bank is one of the worst possible places you could send it. (Why not New Zealand or Australia?) You want to get something like the Spear of Longinus the hell away f rom a place that's in a state of constant war over Abrahamic religion. As far away as possible. Like, say... Antarctica would be ideal. Adam was logically impaled on Lilith's spear -- episode 24 hints at such a primordial battle, through the power of reenactment -- thus Lilith cast Adam's spear out of the White Moon. We see in episode 22 that the spears possess some kind of mysterious impregnation facility, so Adam's spear would be required at the South Pole in order to make the transgenic experiment possible. Lilith's spear is antagonistic to Adam and freezes her, while Adam's own spear enables the research team to impregnate Adam with normally incompatible DNA.
